Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. William Blake, Remote by the Sea 25 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Cherri – programming language that compiles to an Apple Shortuct 187 upvotes, 36 comments
  3. Roulette Computers: Hidden Devices That Predict Spins 26 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Researchers find 3,500-year-old loom that reveals textile revolution 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. An NSFW filter for Marginalia search 71 upvotes, 13 comments
  6. Proactively Parasocial 25 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Fedware: Government apps that spy harder than the apps they ban 244 upvotes, 75 comments
  8. Do your own writing 215 upvotes, 67 comments
  9. How to turn anything into a router 509 upvotes, 188 comments
  10. I use Excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 256 upvotes, 101 comments
  11. Seeing Like a Spreadsheet 36 upvotes, 9 comments
  12. Show HN: Coasts – Containerized Hosts for Agents 41 upvotes, 11 comments
  13. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 178 upvotes, 72 comments
  14. Agents of Chaos 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 96 upvotes, 39 comments
  16. Take better notes, by hand 143 upvotes, 67 comments
  17. America Is Now a Rogue Superpower 66 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. FTC action against Match and OkCupid for deceiving users, sharing personal data 199 upvotes, 104 comments
  19. Turning a MacBook into a touchscreen with $1 of hardware (2018) 82 upvotes, 37 comments
  20. Recover Apple Keychain 26 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Learn Claude Code by doing, not reading 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. A sea of sparks: Seeing radioactivity 37 upvotes, 14 comments
  23. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 244 upvotes, 146 comments
  24. Bird brains (2023) 269 upvotes, 174 comments
  25. OCR for construction documents does not work, we fixed it 92 upvotes, 57 comments
  26. Vulnerability research is cooked 73 upvotes, 47 comments
  27. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 210 upvotes, 177 comments
  29. In math, rigor is vital, but are digitized proofs taking it too far? 81 upvotes, 67 comments
  30. Car Seats as Contraception 11 upvotes, 8 comments

Last generated 5:30am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
